countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: Solidarity surcharge applies at 5.5% of income tax above threshold. Church tax is optional (8-9% of income tax). Social security includes pension (9.3%), health (7.3%), care (1.7%), unemployment (1.3%).

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.notes: Social security is included in the income tax brackets. The 30% ruling allows qualifying expats to receive 30% of salary tax-free for up to 5 years.

specialRules.name: 30% Ruling
specialRules.description: Qualifying expats receive 30% of gross salary tax-free for up to 5 years. Effective tax rate drops significantly.
specialRules.eligible: Recruited from abroad, specific expertise, minimum salary threshold of €46,107 (or €35,048 under 30 with MSc)

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.notes: National Insurance: 8% on earnings between £12,570-£50,270, then 2% above. Personal allowance (£12,570) tapers for income over £100,000.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.notes: Municipal tax averages 32%. State tax of 20% applies to income above SEK 614,000. Employer pays 31.42% social security on top of gross salary. Expert tax relief available for qualifying foreign workers.

specialRules.name: Expert Tax Relief
specialRules.description: Qualifying foreign researchers and key personnel can exempt 25% of salary from income tax for the first 7 years.
specialRules.eligible: Monthly salary above SEK 103,800, or specific research/expert roles

countries.name: Ireland
countries.notes: Universal Social Charge (USC) applies in addition to income tax and PRSI (social security at 4%). Tax credits reduce effective tax rate.

specialRules.name: SARP Relief
specialRules.description: Special Assignee Relief Programme exempts 30% of income above €75,000 from income tax for up to 5 years.
specialRules.eligible: Assigned to Ireland by an employer from abroad, minimum base salary €75,000

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.notes: Federal tax brackets shown. Cantonal and municipal taxes vary significantly (Zurich ~24% total, Zug ~14% total). Social security: AHV/IV/EO 5.3% + ALV 1.1%. Mandatory health insurance is separate (CHF 300-500/month).

countries.name: France
countries.notes: Social security contributions are approximately 22% of gross salary (employee share). France uses a household quotient system for tax calculation. The Passeport Talent visa offers favorable conditions for tech workers.

specialRules.name: Impatriate Regime
specialRules.description: Qualifying expatriates can exempt the relocation premium (up to 50% of total compensation) from income tax for up to 8 years.
specialRules.eligible: Recruited directly from abroad by a French company, not tax resident in France for the previous 5 years

countries.name: Australia
countries.notes: Medicare levy of 2% applies to taxable income. Employer pays 11.5% superannuation (retirement) on top of salary. No social security deductions from employee. Temporary residents are not eligible for the tax-free threshold in the first year.

countries.name: Canada
countries.notes: Federal tax brackets shown. Provincial tax is additional (Ontario ~5-13%, BC ~5-20%, Quebec ~15-25%). CPP contribution is 5.95% up to $68,500. EI premium is 1.58% up to $63,200. Basic personal amount of $15,705 is tax-free.

countries.name: Estonia
countries.notes: Flat 20% income tax rate. Basic exemption of €7,848/year reduces effective rate. Social security: unemployment insurance 1.6% (employee). Employer pays 33% social tax on top of gross salary. One of the lowest tax burdens in the EU.
